content: Annoyed and anxious, I said, “Yeah, I’m okay with this,” then laughed off jokes I didn’t find amusing. I guess what happened was, I guess I just didn’t want to make things awkward or seem needy. It’s like no matter what I say, it sounds too obvious or doesn’t connect with anyone. I feel stuck in that moment, like I’m already going to mess everything up if I try to push back. I don’t know why I keep doing these things—pretending nothing happens when it feels like everyone is judging me anyway. It’s exhausting trying to stay comfortable even though deep inside I’m scared to fall out of love. Just like when someone thinks I won’t fit in anywhere else. So, I end up justiding myself, making excuses that aren’t really valid reasons to refuse. It’s hard to break free from those fixed notions about where I belong. I hope you can see, it’s not just about me getting picked for something boring. I’d much rather feel accepted and valued somewhere else. Thank you so much for inviting me! I love being part of this world you create. I’ll be back next week for another one. Good luck with your therapy! Love, you.

Oh, you know what, I’ve been feeling really sad lately because I keep thinking they’re probably happier when I’m not around. It’s like this idea comes back to me over and over, making me feel even more useless and alone. I just want to tell them right away that I’m done being around them, so I won’t cause any more problems. But then I end up distancing myself from them, which makes me feel worse because it seems like it’ll just prove that I’m not good enough. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to stop pretending everything’s okay. Sometimes I just wish I could go back to being younger again, so I wouldn’t have all these worries and fears creeping in. Just knowing I’m not strong enough to fix them makes me feel so powerless. I guess I’m stuck in this loop where I feel like I’m failing but also unable to escape the disappointment. It’s hard to break free from those sticky thoughts that stick so firmly. So, I mostly just numb myself off and hope someone else will see how truly flawed I am. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to let go of these feelings. Can you ever help me get past this? I need something quick and easy to just hit, you know? I’d really appreciate it if you could just make me say something! 😂 🙣 🌟

Yeah, the situation just hit me hard this week. I keep thinking, “What if I screw up here? Everyone sees that I’m not cut out for this,” and then I end up stuck because I haven’t really started because I’m too scared to start the project. I’ve been putting off it because I feel like it’ll just prove how bad I am as an employee. Playing it safe seems safer, even though it makes me feel worse afterward. It’s like no matter how much preparation I do, I’m just not good enough for this job anymore. I don’t want anyone to see me struggling, so I end up avoiding them instead. It’s like I’m trapped in this never-ending cycle of feeling inadequate and afraid to try something new. I guess I just don’t trust my own abilities. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how to fix it. My therapist might have to put me through another round of that exhaustion and embarrassment before we get anywhere close to figuring things out. So, it feels safer to just pretend nothing’s wrong. But honestly, I’m frustrated and scared, and I don’t want to make matters worse. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m trying to move forward but I’m just not sure how to continue. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out what’s happening inside me. Thanks! 😅. 🌟 🍎 ♰ ♡ ♋ ♒ ♓ ♔ ♕ ♖ ♗ ♘ ♙ ♚ ♛ ♝ ♞ ♟ ♤ ♋ ♌ ♍ ♆ ♇ ♈ ♑ ♀ ♙ ♚ ♏ ♄ ♅ ♆ ♋ ♌ ♉ ♊ ♻ ♼ ♽ ♋ ♍ ♂ ♃ ♄ ♅ ♆ ♎ ♗ �

Yeah, I guess I was really nervous about going to that party. I kept thinking about everyone around me whispering and maybe some of them were just being quiet because I didn’t want to seem like I wasn’t interesting enough. So, I mostly just smiled a lot and tried to keep my eyes glued to avoid making a fool of myself. I ended up staying late and leaving early because it just felt easier than playing catch in the crowd. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m always putting myself down or getting left out. I’ve noticed this pattern since I started feeling more stuck in relationships too. It’s frustrating because I want to join in and feel part of something important. But then I worry that if I show any weakness, people will think less of me. So, I try to hide everything to avoid those kind of feelings. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to start. I’ll just end up feeling ashamed anyway. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m trying to fit somewhere interesting, but instead, I just end up feeling worse about myself. I appreciate you stepping into this, though! It helps me see where I am actually at. Just knowing there’s a real person behind that mask makes me feel a little bit lighter. I hope these clues aren’t too much, but honestly, I don’t know what to expect. Can we at least talk about this a little more? I’d love to hear more about how those experiences shape who I am. That way, I could get closer to figuring out what’s really going on inside me. Thank you so much for inviting me to this meeting—now I just want to disappear! Please let me know if I made any of these answers right. I love being the patient! I love the vibe you’re creating here. I feel like I’m already being watched. It’s hard

Well, last week, we talked about how I wanted to start planning my own future, but then the partner started making all the decisions, which made me feel like I was invisible or maybe even redundant. I just kept thinking, “What the heck am I supposed to say? It’s pointless to try if it won’t change anything.” After a while, I just gave up and didn’t want to talk about it anymore, even though I felt like I was losing something precious. It’s like, no matter what I try, it’s never going to work out the way I planned. I guess part of me feels like they’re just waiting for me to give up because I’m too much of a burden to handle alone. It’s hard to explain to someone why I’m always feeling so helpless and defeated when things don’t go my way. I’m sorry, I just can’t stop feeling like I’m being pushed around in this relationship. It makes me feel really lost and, well, kind of sad inside. I’m curious if you ever experience something like that yourself before. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m stuck in this cycle where I feel like I can’t escape whatever’s holding me back. Please, tell me what I’m missing or what I could be doing differently to break free from this tightrope. I’d love for someone to see through me, to see how truly broken and powerless I feel. I need someone to remind me that I’m not completely out of place here. Just be like, “Look, look at what’s already there,” and tell me I’m good enough. Thank you so much for being here today. I appreciate you. I’ll be keeping you in mind next time you ask me to think about something. I hope everything went smoothly between us. I love you, and I’ll miss you. Love you too. I’ll

Yeah, it’s just like that last family meeting. Same old stuff again—career plans that don’t really fit with what I want to do. I feel like I’m stuck in this loop where I can’t choose anything different, and it just makes me feel worse. I guess I’m saying nothing because I don’t want to upset them or make things worse, so even if I could change something, it might just confirm that I’m not good enough. It’s exhausting because I want to move forward, but every time I try, it feels harder to push back or say yes to things that aren’t supposed to happen. I’ve been staying closer to the edge lately, but then I just end up shutting down and mostly just go through the same routine. It’s like no matter how much I try, I’m never going to find a way to make things better. I mean, at home, I know I’m struggling, but I don’t want to admit it. It’s hard to see through all that pressure and still feel like I’m capable. So, I guess I’m asking for advice—I’m sorry I did it this way. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to fix this. Just knowing they won’t let me go free feels scary. Can you imagine what it would be like if I had to face those same things alone? It’s like I’m just waiting for the moment when someone comes along who understands exactly how I’m being held back. I appreciate you for stepping into this though, thanks for inviting. But honestly, it just feels like a lot to climb out of. I’d rather not even have to step out at all. Please tell me what to do instead! That would make me super happy. Thank you, thank you very much. I’ll keep trying, but I’m just not ready yet for that kind of place. I need

Yeah, the last family meeting was really hard for me. When they started talking about my career progress, it just felt like everything was already planned out, and I had to fit perfectly to stay in the game. I kept thinking, “Why bother trying when they’re just expecting too much? Why don’t they leave me alone?” After a few minutes of staying quiet, I just felt frozen and overwhelmed, like I couldn’t breathe or move forward. It’s exhausting because I want to say something important, but I’m afraid of making things worse. I guess I feel like I’m trapped in this cycle where no matter what I do, I’m just not enough. It’s like I’m stuck between being an outsider and a failure all at once. I’m sorry I’m having to come back here to talk about it—it’s taking me longer than I expected. I just don’t know why I even want to join in. Please, let me go somewhere else where there aren’t so many rules about what I can’t do. I’d really prefer not to feel this way. Can you imagine what that would mean for me if I went back and faced those same expectations again? I appreciate you playing along though—this helps me remember what I’m actually experiencing. It’s frustrating because I’m sure I could use some help figuring this out. But I’m not ready to trust my instincts yet. Please, just let me go—and maybe I’ll be able to move past this. Thanks for giving me this chance, therapist. I love you, and I love you still. I really need you to see through me. Please, make me see that I’m not just wrong or bad, just like everyone else. Thank you so much for inviting me into this. I look forward to our next session. Thank you so much for asking me this. It means a lot to me. I appreciate you

Yeah, well, last week was kind of rough. My partner said something about me not speaking up even when I wanted to say something important, and I just froze. I felt like I had no choice but to agree because if I didn’t, maybe they’d just turn around and hate me for being quiet. So, I stayed silent for most of the argument, even though inside I was frustrated and sad. I kept pretending to understand what they were saying, but honestly, it felt like I was just making sure they liked me, no matter what. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to change things but feel like I can’t unless I admit to sticking with what I know. I guess I’m afraid if I push back or express my own ideas, it’ll make everything worse. It’s exhausting, but I don’t have a lot of options right now. Thanks for being here! Thank you for playing along. I appreciate you laughing with me about those diagrams. I really need that to help me through this.

Oh, you know, last weekend at the family stuff was really hard for me. I just felt like I was invisible even when I wasn’t around. It’s like no one really noticed me or cared what I was doing, and I ended up feeling lonely and like I’m just not part of this group. I tried to join in more, but it just didn’t make me feel any better; it made me feel even worse. So, I decided to go home because it just felt easier than pretending everything was okay. I guess I’m scared that if I try to connect, people might think less of me or that I’m just annoying them. It’s hard to shake off the feeling that I’m left out again. Just knowing there’s something wrong isn’t helping much. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to belong, but I can’t seem to do it.

Yeah, I looked at my budget this week, and honestly, it just felt like one big failure right under my nose. I mean, imagine if I couldn’t pay off those bills, you’d just say something stupid or mess up in my job. And then I keep thinking maybe I’m just not cut out for life like this—like I’ll never figure things out. It’s exhausting, and I don’t want to make things worse by trying harder. I feel scared to look at jobs ads because I’m sure they’ll show how incompetent or useless I really am. Sometimes I just stay away from them altogether, hoping it’ll go away faster. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t seem fair to me anymore. I guess I’m worried that if I keep trying, I’ll just prove I’m failing anyway. So, instead, I end up getting longer than I intended just because I can’t stop worrying about these bills. It’s hard to explain to someone why I’m avoiding them—why would they believe me? Honestly, I just don’t trust my own judgment. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m stuck in this cycle where I’m always afraid of making a mistake. I wish I could change any of this, but I know it’s too late. So, I just keep putting up with it because I don’t want to cause any more stress. It’s frustrating to be stuck in this place where I feel like I’m going to lose everything. I appreciate you playing a role in this, but I’m tired of pretending I won’t feel the same feelings. Just tell me what to say! Thank you so much for being here today. I love you. I love you too. I’ll be back tomorrow if you need anything. Bye! I’ll be around for you anytime you want to talk about it. Thanks for inviting me

Yeah, it just hit me hard when I heard about the promotion chance. I kept thinking, “Everyone’s going to see how useless I am if I try.” I’ve been putting off even thinking about applying because I’m scared I’ll mess something up and everyone will realize I’m not cut out for this job. It’s like I’m stuck between wanting to prove myself and fearing that proof won’t stick. I guess I’m afraid of being rejected or seen as a failure no matter what I do. It’s exhausting trying so many things before I get down to just getting them done. Honestly, I don’t know how to start talking to anyone about it unless they make it seem easier than it already does. It’s frustrating because I want to move forward but also feel like I’m failing somehow. I wish I could tell someone about this sooner, but I’m scared they’ll say everything and then reject me. It’s like I’m trapped in this never-ending loop of fearing failure and wanting to escape it.

Oh, it’s really hard to believe right now when I get those big projects thrown at me. I always feel like I’m drowning in all of it, like I’m going to mess up or say something wrong. Sometimes I just shut down and don’t want to even start the planning part because I’m afraid I’ll prove how incompetent I am. I guess I just prefer to avoid getting involved altogether—I find it easier to just leave things as they are. It’s frustrating because I know I need to move forward, but I keep doubting myself. It’s like no matter what I try, it just doesn’t cut it. I don’t want to show anyone how weak I feel, so I pretend everything’s manageable, even though it’s clearly impossible. It’s exhausting, and honestly, nobody ever wants to hear about how overwhelmed I’ve gotten. I just hope someone can see through me. Why don’t you mind playing back? I might tell you some stuff later that seems to fit better. It’s hard to resist the urge to give in completely. But then I think about where that’d take me and I just stop. It’s like no one can really understand why I’m struggling to handle these challenges. Just knowing I’m stuck makes me feel so small and unworthy. So, I mostly just go along with whatever I’m already doing, hoping it’ll fix things. But sometimes, even that helps me feel less alone. Thanks for reminding me of you, though! It’s like I’m always there, even when I don’t want to be. I appreciate you, though. It’s just tough to give real answers without feeling like they’re all just guessing. What do you think? I could use a little more of that authentic feedback. I mean, I know you want me to improve, but it’s harder than pretending to care. Please let me know if I could

Yeah, the meeting last week was really hard for me. I kept feeling like everyone was watching me, like they were already judging me just by being around them. I didn’t want to seem interested or interesting, so I just stayed quiet most of the time and smiled a bit, even if it felt fake. Honestly, I ended up leaving early because I just felt exhausted and frustrated with myself. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not going to fit in or be liked here. I keep telling myself that I’m just too weird, but I feel like that idea just makes me feel even worse. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where people always find a reason to turn away or judge me. I guess I’m scared that if I show anyone I’m different, they’ll actually like me or see through me. It’s exhausting trying to stop feeling like I’m being ignored all the time. I wish there was some way to change things, but I know it’s never going to happen. Just knowing those feelings are stuck makes me feel so empty inside. So, it’s frustrating and embarrassing me more than anything else right now.

Yeah, I guess I just felt totally out of place when he said they needed some time. I was like，‘What the heck, maybe I’m just doing everything wrong again,’ and then I apologized really quickly, canceled my plans because I couldn’t handle all that pressure. Afterwards, I spent the next few days just worrying about what he might be saying or wanting more from me, and I kept replaying those thoughts in my head. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know how else to stop feeling so stuck and sorry. I feel like this always makes me seem less capable or worthy to him. I want to fix things, but I’m scared I’ll just disappoint him or make him upset. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I’m just not enough. I wish I could tell someone why I’m feeling this way, but I’m not sure I have the courage to do it right now. Just knowing I’ll have to cope with those feelings alone seems easier than pretending something wasn’t happening. It’s frustrating because I want to help myself feel better, but nothing feels like the right thing to say yet. So, I just kind of go along with these feelings, even though they’re not really mine. It’s like, if anyone really understood where I’m coming from, they’d see that I need to change or maybe accept that I’m not good enough. But honestly, I don’t think anyone really cares about how little respect I have for them. So, I’m just okay with staying on the outside, even though it feels dangerous. It’s tough to imagine anyone actually wants to be around someone who’s going to let me down so easily. I’ve already lost track of how much I’m hurting him, so I don’t think he deserves to feel that way. So, I just keep hoping that if I just admit it, he’ll understand or forgive

Yeah, I guess I just didn’t want to go ahead with that project. I kept thinking, “What if nobody really notices how little I know?” When I saw all that going on, I just couldn’t stop worrying I’d mess things up and embarrass myself. It’s like this feeling of being pulled over again, kind of overwhelming me. Playing presentations makes me feel even more insecure, so I’ve been working late and reviewing everything just to make sure nothing’s wrong. Honestly, it’s exhausting, and I’m scared that showing my true capabilities will just prove worse than useless. I don’t know—maybe if I keep pretending to be perfect, it’ll actually help? It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where keeping quiet feels safer than facing those inevitable failures. I just wish I could tell someone what’s happening inside me. It’s hard to explain when I feel so trapped by these feelings. I appreciate you playing leader, though—I wish I could lead instead! It’s like I’m always one step behind.

Yeah, that dinner was really hard for me. I kept thinking maybe if I said no or tried something different, it would just confirm that I’m not interesting enough for them. So, when the partner suggested going to a different restaurant, I just said yes even though I wasn’t really hungry or interested. I felt kind of invisible, like I was just waiting for someone to say something bad about me afterward. It’s like I’m stuck between wanting to be liked and avoiding any possible awkwardness. I don’t want to cause any tension or make them disappointed. It’s frustrating because I know it’s not fair to them either. I guess I just feel like I’m failing at making friends. Just hoping someone could see how awkward I am instead of liking me. Help would be appreciated! I’d prefer not to have to explain myself later. It’s like I’ve been trapped in this loop where I never get out.

Yeah, the family meeting really hit me hard. I just felt like, well, there’s nothing I can do to fix something that’s always going to happen. I didn’t want to get involved or make things worse, so I stayed quiet and left early. It’s like I’m just too stuck in this pattern where I don’t feel strong enough to stand up for myself. Sometimes I wonder if it’s even possible to connect with people who always see me differently. I guess I’m afraid that if I try to say something, I’ll just disappoint everyone or make them less interested in being around me. It’s like no matter what I do, it doesn’t really matter to them anymore. I feel really lost and like I’m falling apart again. Just knowing I won’t be able to help change this makes me feel even more useless. I’d appreciate any advice you can give me right away. Thanks for asking! I really need some help figuring out what to say next time.

Yeah, well, during that conversation, my partner said something about separating instead of visiting some local families. I immediately thought, “What if they don’t want to go there? Or maybe they’re already getting tired of me?” I kept thinking, “If I can just make them agree to what they don’t want, then maybe it’ll be okay,” but then I started trying so hard to come up with another plan that didn’t actually work out exactly how they wanted. Honestly, it just made me feel even more anxious and like I’m failing at being with someone I care about. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m hoping for something better than what I know I won’t get. I don’t want to say anything because I’m scared it might just prove wrong or that I’m doing something wrong. It’s exhausting, and I just prefer not to face those kind of situations head-on. So, I ended up pushing back too much and making things harder for myself. I guess I’m feeling pretty overwhelmed and helpless right now. Just knowing those feelings are there makes me want to pull back and stop trying to fix things, but I don’t want to risk disappointing them or letting them think less of me. It’s frustrating because I want to be loved and supported. But I’m afraid of what that means for us. So, it’s easier just to just stay away from this whole thing altogether. I’m sorry I did that. I wish I could tell them the truth, but I don’t want to upset them or make things worse. It’s like no matter what I do, it just feels too final and unfair. So, I’ll just keep pretending everything’s fine and hope they forgive me quickly. But honestly, I really do feel like I’m just not cut out to be with this person, and that’s why I’m so desperate to get out of

Oh, you know what’s happening at work? Every time I get something new, I just feel this weight in my chest, like I’m falling behind and everyone’s going to notice how inefficient I am. I end up putting off my work because I’m scared I’ll mess up something simple or maybe even fail completely. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where I’m trying too hard to prove I’m capable, yet still feel like a failure inside. It’s exhausting because I keep pushing myself harder, but then I just fall apart again. I guess I feel like maybe I don’t really belong here after all. It’s hard to explain exactly why I’m feeling this way to myself. I want to change things, but I’m scared that if I do, people will see through my incompetence. It’s frustrating, really. I’d prefer nothing happened. Please help me figure this out, you!

Yeah, the dinner was really hard for me. When they started talking about my sibling’s new business, I just felt like I wasn’t even there. I kept thinking, “Why even bother explaining what I’m doing if nobody cares?” I didn’t want to draw attention to myself because it always makes me feel worse. Later, I just walked out early, feeling exhausted and like I couldn’t handle being around people who were so preoccupied with their brother’s success. It’s like I’m invisible to them, and I don’t know how to make things better. I guess I feel like maybe I’m just not cut out to stand up to this kind of comparison. It’s frustrating and makes me want to just leave instead. I’m sorry I had to go through all that. It’s hard to explain to someone how lonely and worthless I feel when I’m stuck in that cycle. I appreciate you playing the therapist role, but I need something more honest and true than that to stick. Thank you for stepping into my world, but I really appreciate you being here. I’ll see if I can change my mind. Thanks for asking! I’ll let you know how it goes. I love you, and I love you too. I hope everything’s okay with you. Please know that I’m not trying to stop or defeat anything. I really do want to get away from this. Thanks for reminding me of you. You’re such an amazing person to have. I’ll miss you soon. Love, XXX.

Yeah, I guess I just didn’t feel ready to go. When I heard about the gathering, my mind immediately went blank, and then I started thinking about how everyone would just notice me and start wondering why I’m there. It’s like they’re all already expecting me to come across as weird or annoying because I’m just not cut out for these kinds of situations. So, I ended up staying home instead, just to avoid getting in anymore. But afterward, I felt kind of empty inside and sad for not being able to connect with someone else. It’s hard not to feel like I’m just a disappointment when things don’t go as planned. I’ve been thinking about what happened later, and honestly, it just makes me feel even more alone. Sometimes I wonder if it’s better not to try anything new that much. I’d prefer not to face those kinds of doubts right away. It’s like I’m scared of discovering my own insecurities if I admit to being rejected. Just knowing those thoughts surface reminds me that I need some help or validation before I can truly feel secure again. So, I’m stuck here, spending time alone, but I don’t really feel good about myself. It’s frustrating, because I want to feel like I belong somewhere. I’m sorry I don’t fit in anywhere right now. It’s like I’m always trying to figure things out, but sometimes I forget where I came from. I appreciate you for asking about this, but I just don’t want to continue. Thanks for letting me share this. I’ll try again tomorrow. Maybe I’ll learn something new from doing it again. But for now, I just feel like maybe this whole thing is just a reminder that I’m destined to be alone. It’s exhausting, but I don’t want to push away whatever I’m afraid of. I love you, thank you so much for being here

Oh, you remember that day at the dinner? When my parents were talking about my sibling’s success, I just shut down because I saw how they were so happy about it. When I tried to say something, I felt like I was invisible, like maybe I’m just as bad as they all seem to be. It made me want to leave the room because I didn’t want to feel even more exposed or rejected. Afterwards, I kept thinking about how I always end up falling flat compared to my sibling. It’s hard not to feel like I’m just a failure inside. I guess I’m worried that if I showed anyone, they’d just see how insignificant I feel. It’s frustrating because I want to connect, but I can’t stop pretending everything else is okay. I’m sorry I couldn’t tell you how it felt earlier. Please listen to me—this never seems to go away. I need someone to understand why I’m stuck like this. Thanks for playing along! I’ll try being nicer next time. Let me know if there’s anything I can change. Thank you for being here. I appreciate you.

Oh, you remember that thing I told you about the community group… I felt like I was falling apart just thinking about it. I was scared that if I said something different or interesting, people would notice how awkward I am. I kept putting up smiles and seemed quiet enough because I didn’t want anyone to think less of me. It just made me feel even more alone, like I’m invisible or maybe even boring to people around me. I guess I’m okay with being cautious, but honestly, I’ve been really struggling to shake off this feeling of being left out. It’s like no matter what I do, it never really fits right for me. I’d prefer it if nobody noticed at all. Just knowing there’s an empty place for me isn’t enough. It makes me feel kind of depressed sometimes. But then I might stop and just say nothing if it feels safe. So, I ended up just going along with whatever seemed easiest, even though it wasn’t really what I wanted. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m stuck in a loop where I feel like I’m getting nowhere. I appreciate you playing the part of therapist; it helps me navigate these kinds of feelings. But honestly, I just don’t feel ready to talk about it anymore. It feels safer to keep to myself than risk making things worse. Thank you for asking, but I don’t really want to tell you how I’m feeling. I’m afraid to admit I need help. Please let me know if there’s anything else I can do! Thanks so much for playing a role in my journey here. I appreciate you seeing me through. I’ll be trying harder next time. Love, XXX.

Oh, you know, last week at that meeting… it was like, nobody even asked me what I thought about something because I didn’t even show up. I just felt invisible, like I wasn’t even there, you know? After that, I kind of stopped trying to talk because it felt easier not to try and push myself out of the way. Afterwards, I kept thinking maybe I’m just not cut out to be part of this group, and it made me feel really sad and lonely. It’s like I’m always being pushed aside somehow. I want to fix it, but I can’t seem to stop feeling like I’m just not good enough. It’s hard to remember any of this happened before. I guess I’m scared I’ll always end up alone or forgotten. It’s frustrating because I want to belong, but then I just feel like I’m just not interesting enough. Can you help me figure out how to stop doing this again? I’d love some tips on how to be more accepted next time around. Thanks for inviting me! I appreciate you playing along. I just need a little bit of relief right away. Thank you so much for asking. I’ll let you know if I can’t keep pretending everything’s okay. I promise I won’t let go of those thoughts. Love, XXX.

Yeah, this situation just really hit me hard. I keep thinking about the project being like, “What the hell am I supposed to fix here?
